title: Nobody loves Ritsuko
wikitext:
[]: Nobody loves Ritsuko             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Mon Nov 12, 2012 8:22 am Something I have noticed, nobody seems to notice her too much and there isn't much rave going on around her. She's kinda just there to flesh out Gendo some more. Even in her key scene in EoE I couldn't help but feel super indifferent nonetheless. The question is, was that pure intention on Anno's part?

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Mon Nov 12, 2012 8:29 am I always saw her as the woman Gendo boinks in the meantime. otherwise she's pretty forgettable.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Nov 12, 2012 8:53 am She's there to be the obligatory scientist in the secret organization. When something doesn't go right it's her job to offer big sciencey reasons why. Also to offer an equal position relationship for Misato so she can have conversation scenes since Kaji doesn't show up till later, dies early, and is the love interest, Shinji's her subordinate, and Gendo's her senior. Also pretty sure she's Tine's waifu, though that might be too strong a word.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon Nov 12, 2012 9:07 am I have love for Ritsy and her fine fine legs of science. She just never gets treated right.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Nov 12, 2012 9:14 am this thread is indirectly my fault isn't it?

[]:             Quote Post by Tankred » Mon Nov 12, 2012 9:26 am Xard wrote: this thread is indirectly my fault isn't it?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Nov 12, 2012 9:47 am InstrumentalityOne wrote: The question is, was that pure intention on Anno's part?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Nov 12, 2012 10:13 am Tankred wrote: Spurring on IO? Probably, but yeah, legs of science and hair dye.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Mon Nov 12, 2012 10:25 am Xard wrote: I was thinking about suspiciously familiar form of thread title

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Mon Nov 12, 2012 10:31 am In her screentime we never see the personal and emotional aspects of her life so that is one reason alone why most people dont care about her.... For me i always thought her being in the shadow of Misato probably because Misato is a character tha tries to live her life carefree(despite having unresolved family and social issues)although i always thought Ritsuko could turn to an interesting character....

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon Nov 12, 2012 10:45 am well...um...she's always nice to Ibuki. Some on-the-job training too. Meanwhile Misato takes advantage of the bridge bunny with a crush on her and sends him out to do laundry and whatnot. And she is always trying to pair Misato and Kaji back up even though she could clearly have Kaji for herself.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Mon Nov 12, 2012 10:59 am Ritsuko is just passive aggressive towards the Ikari men. With gun scene in EoE Eva being the exception then again I really don't like Ritsuko.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Mon Nov 12, 2012 11:40 am It's the MOLE

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Mon Nov 12, 2012 12:04 pm Well, I do like Ritsuko. Everybody should love her.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Nov 12, 2012 12:17 pm Aww... Ritsuko is great, she just suffers from making poor relationship choices and having to be the voice of reason in a show where miracle plot contrivances happen all the time.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Nov 12, 2012 12:31 pm I think she fully serves her purpose in terms of plot/constrasting/message/analysis of 21st century women. She's just not very likeable, but that doesn't equal her being the result of bad writing - it's the purely emotional/subjective Part she doesn't attract much. After all, she's fake and ultimately submissive in a stupid way. So while few people are particularly crazy about her in the sense that they find her hot or woobietastic, most of those who don't don't neccessarily want her removed from the show - Aoba and Hyuuga don't exactly have giant fanclubs, either. I guess those who do like her find her compelling as someone who was "corrupted" (by Gendo) and proceeds to spread that corruption to others (Telling Maya to ignore her conscience etc)

[]:             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Mon Nov 12, 2012 1:37 pm In some way or another, I like all the characters in the series. They have traits that I like as well as traits that I can relate to as well as traits I don't like. Of all the characters, Ritsuko and Shinji are the ones I can relate to the most mainly because at some point in my life, I was just like them. Even today I share more traits than I care to admit with Ritsuko. As a result, I can deeply sympathize with her as well as care about her character, even with as little as is presented in the series concerning her personal life. Now, I can't say she is my favorite character, as I really don't have a favorite character in the series, but she is one that I can relate to more than most and I like her more than many other characters.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Nov 12, 2012 1:43 pm That's not corruption, that's pragmatism. Maya's plucky idealism is endearing but it's also extremely naive. It also doesn't make much sense for her character, tbh. Why is she against an AI pilot but practically gushes about the idea that the Magi run the city government?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Nov 12, 2012 1:44 pm Dima wrote: In her screentime we never see the personal and emotional aspects of her life so that is one reason alone why most people dont care about her....

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Mon Nov 12, 2012 2:43 pm NemZ wrote: That's not corruption, that's pragmatism. Maya's plucky idealism is endearing but it's also extremely naive. It also doesn't make much sense for her character, tbh. Why is she against an AI pilot but practically gushes about the idea that the Magi run the city government?
summary:
[]: Nobody loves Ritsuko             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Mon Nov 12, 2012 8:22 am Something I have noticed, nobody seems to notice her too much and there isn't much rave going on around her. She's kinda just there to flesh out Gendo some more. Even in her key scene in EoE I couldn't help but feel super indifferent nonetheless. The question is, was that pure intention on Anno's part?

[]:    &nbs
